# Git Cheat Sheet — Branches & Commits

Copy-paste. Swap `<feature-slug>` and `<task-slug>` for what you're doing. Everything else is literal.

**Commit messages are generated by Claude** via your `prepare-commit-msg` hook — so `git commit` with no `-m` is the normal case. Only use `git commit -m "..."` when you want to override the generated message (e.g. empty commits to kick off a branch).

---

## Legend — what to swap

- `<feature-slug>` — lowercase kebab, matches a Notion Feature. Examples: `auth-users`, `architecture-setup`, `chat`, `home-dashboard`
- `<task-slug>` — ≤ 5 words, lowercase kebab. Examples: `login-screen`, `version-catalog`, `push-notifications`

---

## Pattern A — one PR to main (small change)

```
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>
git commit --allow-empty -m "chore: start <task-slug>"
git push -u origin feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>
```

**Work. Stage. Commit (Claude fills the message).**

```
git add .
git commit
git push
```

**Open the PR — Claude fills the body too.**

```
gh pr create --base main --fill
```

Then squash-merge on GitHub. Delete the branch.

---

## Pattern B — nested: parent + sub-branches (big feature)

Use for Auth, Chat, anything >1 PR.

### 1. Parent branch (once per Feature)

```
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/<feature-slug>
git push -u origin feature/<feature-slug>
```

No `--`, no Notion Task. The parent is just a git grouping.

### 2. Sub-branch from parent (repeat per sub-task)

```
git checkout feature/<feature-slug>
git pull
git checkout -b feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>
git commit --allow-empty -m "chore: start <task-slug>"
git push -u origin feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>
```

Notion Task created at `🔨 In Progress`, linked to Feature.

### 3. Work → commit → push

```
git add .
git commit
git push
```

### 4. Open sub-PR — base is the parent branch, NOT main

```
gh pr create --base feature/<feature-slug> --fill
```

Task → `👀 In Review`. Claude writes the body. Merge on GitHub. Task → `✅ Done`.

### 5. Keep parent fresh with main (periodic, from parent branch)

```
git checkout feature/<feature-slug>
git fetch origin
git merge origin/main
git push
```

### 6. Ship parent to main (once all sub-tasks are merged)

```
git checkout feature/<feature-slug>
git pull
gh pr create --base main --fill
```

Merge on GitHub. Then cleanup:

```
git checkout main && git pull
git branch -D feature/<feature-slug>
git push origin --delete feature/<feature-slug>
```

---

## Concrete example — filled in

Starting Module 6 Auth, first sub-task is the login screen.

**Parent (once):**

```
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feature/auth-users
git push -u origin feature/auth-users
```

**First sub-task — login screen:**

```
git checkout feature/auth-users
git pull
git checkout -b feature/auth-users--login-screen
git commit --allow-empty -m "chore: start login-screen"
git push -u origin feature/auth-users--login-screen
# ...work...
git add .
git commit
git push
gh pr create --base feature/auth-users --fill
```

**Next sub-task — signup flow:**

```
git checkout feature/auth-users
git pull                                          # picks up merged login-screen
git checkout -b feature/auth-users--signup-flow
git commit --allow-empty -m "chore: start signup-flow"
git push -u origin feature/auth-users--signup-flow
# ...work...
git add .
git commit
git push
gh pr create --base feature/auth-users --fill
```

**Final — parent to main (after all sub-tasks merged):**

```
git checkout feature/auth-users
git pull
gh pr create --base main --fill
```

---

## Useful status commands

```
git status                                        # what's staged / unstaged
git branch                                        # which branches exist locally
git log --oneline -10                             # last 10 commits
git branch -a                                     # local + remote branches
gh pr list                                        # open PRs in this repo
gh pr status                                      # PRs involving you
```

---

## Recovery commands (when something goes wrong)

**Wrong branch — move uncommitted changes to a new branch:**

```
git stash
git checkout -b feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>
git stash pop
```

**Committed to main by accident — move the commit to a new branch:**

```
git branch feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>    # save current state as new branch
git reset --hard origin/main                      # reset main to remote
git checkout feature/<feature-slug>--<task-slug>
```

**Pushed to wrong remote branch — delete remote, push to correct one:**

```
git push origin --delete <wrong-branch>
git branch -m <wrong-branch> <correct-branch>
git push -u origin <correct-branch>
```

**Claude-generated commit message is wrong / you want to edit it:**

The hook opens your editor with the message pre-filled. Edit it, save, quit — git commits with whatever you save. If the editor already closed and you want to fix the last commit:

```
git commit --amend                                # edit message of last commit
git push --force-with-lease                       # only if already pushed
```

**Skip the hook entirely for one commit (override with explicit message):**

```
git commit -m "<your message>"
```

**Zsh prompt shows `quote>` or `dquote>`:**

Press **Ctrl+C**. An unclosed `'` or `"` in a command — zsh is waiting for you to close the quote.

---

## Branch types recap

| Prefix | Notion Task? | Use for |
|---|---|---|
| `feature/<slug>--<task>` | ✅ yes | Feature work |
| `bug/<slug>--<task>` | ✅ yes | Bug fixes under a Feature |
| `improve/<slug>--<task>` | ✅ yes | Refactors / improvements under a Feature |
| `feature/<slug>` (no `--`) | ❌ no (intentional) | Parent branch for Pattern B |
| `fix/…`, `chore/…`, `docs/…` | ❌ no | One-off changes that don't belong to a Feature |
